rationale: Post-Φ-B5 doctrine now freezes distributed authority meaning, authority regions, handoff boundaries, proof-anchor and quorum subject classes, and distributed invalidity categories.
The earlier Φ-B4 and Υ-D doctrine already froze hotswap boundaries, live trust-transition prerequisites, live-cutover receipt continuity, and publication/trust operationalization.
Ζ-P planning can therefore reconcile blockers and execution gates against explicit doctrine instead of inventing missing runtime or trust semantics ad hoc.
The remaining open work is now blocker reconciliation, prerequisite matrixing, and execution-gate freezing for Ζ rather than another missing doctrine family.
Any additional Υ-D3-style work would now be mostly consolidation rather than a prerequisite to pre-Ζ admission.
Distributed authority, trust-transition prerequisites, cutover receipts, and publication/trust operationalization are now explicit.
Ζ itself remains blocked by proof, convergence, cutover, and live-system realization work that Ζ-P must first reconcile and gate.

The repo now has explicit doctrine for coexistence, hotswap boundaries, distributed authority foundations, live trust-transition prerequisites, live-cutover receipt continuity, and publication/trust operationalization.
That completed doctrine packet is strong enough to support a bounded planning-and-admission band for Ζ.
Blueprint readiness surfaces still classify distributed shard relocation and other live operations as unrealistic or not implemented, which remains correct for Ζ proper.
cautions: Ready status applies to the pre-Ζ admission band only.
This does not admit Ζ live operations, distributed relocation implementation, proof-anchor quorum execution, or live trust execution systems.
Ζ-P must preserve distributed-authority law, hotswap boundaries, trust prerequisites, live-cutover receipt continuity, and release-control doctrine.

rationale: Controller-specific alignment now mainly consolidates already-frozen doctrine rather than closing a distinct blocker family before Ζ-P.
This work is better deferred until after Ζ-P reveals whether any controller-specific residue remains real.
The remaining open work is blocker reconciliation and gate freezing for Ζ, not another missing runtime or control-plane constitutional layer.
Another narrow band before Ζ-P would mostly restate distinctions already explicit across Φ-B5 and Υ-D0..Υ-D2.

zeta_blockers_or_prerequisites.reason: Ζ-P0..Ζ-P2 have not yet reconciled the surviving blocker set into one explicit admission packet, prerequisite matrix, and execution-gate surface.
Φ-B5 freezes subject and boundary classes, but proof-backed replication and lawful state-partition transfer remain unproven.
Replay, cross-shard log, checkpoint, and proof-anchor classes are explicit, but distributed continuity proof and verification criteria are not yet frozen for Ζ.
Hotswap and distributed-authority law are explicit, but runtime cutover proof across those boundaries remains unresolved.
Φ-B5 now names trust-convergence subjects, but later work still has to show how distributed authority and trust posture converge lawfully.
Admission, prerequisite, and operationalization doctrine is explicit, but live trust, revocation, and publication systems remain unrealized.
Generalized live-cutover receipt law is explicit, but later Ζ work still needs real receipt realization rather than doctrine alone.

must_avoid_replacing: active checkpoint law with stale blueprint drift or older numbering
runtime or release doctrine with shard, net, CI, or tooling folklore
trust truth with mirror, archive, publication, or local verifier convenience
blocker evidence with optimistic live-ops assumptions

ownership_projection_cautions: fields/ remains canonical semantic field substrate while field/ remains transitional
schema/ remains canonical semantic contract law while schemas/ remains projection-facing
packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging and activation scope while data/packs/ remains scoped authored-pack authority
canonical prose and semantic specs outrank projected or generated mirrors
the thin runtime/ root is not canonical by name alone
release, archive, mirror, publication, trust, distributed, and tooling convenience must not infer canon or permission

reason: Φ-B5 plus the prior Φ-B4 and Υ-D band closes the last missing doctrine gap before a pre-Ζ admission band can be entered honestly.
The repo now has enough runtime, trust, cutover, receipt, and release law to reconcile surviving blockers without inventing new meaning.
Another pre-Ζ-P narrow band would mostly consolidate already-explicit law rather than reduce a distinct blocker family.

stability.replacement_target: later post-Ζ-P checkpoint may refine readiness judgments without replacing the checkpoint law frozen here
